title: Tanker report - Week 34
summary:
Clean
Clean Petroleum Product freight saw a resurgence in the Middle East Gulf. TC1 has jumped up 50 WS points to WS266.88 taking the TCE for the run up over $55,000 per day. Similarly, a run west on an LR2, TC20 has firmed up about $770,000 to just shy of $5,000,000.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: Clean Petroleum Product freight saw a resurgence in the Middle East Gulf. TC1 has jumped up 50 WS points to WS266.88 taking the TCE for the run up over $55,000 per day.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: VLCC rates have remained flat over the week across all regions compared to a relatively volatile market previously. For 270,000mt Middle East Gulf/China, the rate through the week was flat with only a marginal rise after initially dropping at the front end to WS79.64 and ending up at WS80.64 (a round-trip TCE of $37,270 per day).

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: The biggest mover by far was the 135,000mt Black Sea/Augusta market, rising just over WS18 points from an initial WS172.78 to WS188.72 (An increase of $10,540 per day TCE round trip; $64,555 to $75,095 per day). This far outweighed the quieter Middle East Gulf/Med or West African/Continent markets which dipped both just over WS2 points from the start of the week.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The Med Aframax market has begun moving upward with the rate for 80,000mt Ceyhan/West Mediterranean improving by WS8.87 to WS194.06 (a round-trip TCE of $44,474 per day). This is repeated in the North where 80,000mt Hound Point/UK Continent rose by nearly WS16 points and now sits at WS193.13 (a daily round-trip TCE of $53,482).
